notes:
xếp hàng = to line up / queue (stand in a line and wait your turn).  

It’s a verb, used when people wait in order for something (buying, checking in, entering somewhere). Usually followed by a place or purpose.  

Close synonym: xếp hàng đợi (same meaning, just a bit more explicit). Opposite idea: chen lấn (to push/shove instead of lining up properly).
exampleSentences: Sáng nay em phải xếp hàng rất lâu để mua bánh mì.
Chị thường xếp hàng tại siêu thị vào cuối tuần.
Anh và bạn bè đã xếp hàng trước rạp chiếu phim từ sớm.
exampleSentencesTranslated: This morning I had to queue for a long time to buy bread.
She usually queues at the supermarket on weekends.
He and his friends queued in front of the cinema early.
notes_md:
**xếp hàng** = to line up / queue (stand in a line and wait your turn).  

It’s a verb, used when people wait in order for something (buying, checking in, entering somewhere). Usually followed by a place or purpose.  


Close synonym: **xếp hàng đợi** (same meaning, just a bit more explicit). Opposite idea: **chen lấn** (to push/shove instead of lining up properly).

definitions.vi: Đứng thành hàng để chờ đợi theo thứ tự.

example.vi: Mọi người đang xếp hàng mua vé.
example.en: People are lining up to buy tickets.
example.es: La gente está haciendo fila para comprar boletos.

content.sentence: Chúng tôi phải ___ để mua vé.

translation.en: We have to line up to buy tickets.
Students are lining up to enter the class.
